playlists.title: Exploitation & Labor
playlists.description: Classic surplus-value and wage arguments. Timed challenge rounds.

prompts.text: Capitalism exploits the working class — profit is unpaid labor stolen from workers.
prompts.hint: Cite subjective value, risk, and coordination — not vibes.

prompts.text: Billionaires should not exist. Nobody needs that much wealth.
prompts.hint: Separate consumption from equity claims; mobility and absolute gains.

prompts.text: CEOs are parasites who extract value while workers create everything.
prompts.hint: Residual claimancy, firm theory, and market discipline on excess pay.

playlists.title: Nordic Model Myths
playlists.description: Democratic socialism vs Nordic market economies.

prompts.text: Sweden and Denmark prove democratic socialism works — we should copy them.
prompts.hint: Private ownership, school choice, flexible labor, trade openness.

prompts.text: If America had Nordic taxes we would finally have justice.
prompts.hint: Tax mix, VAT, and pre-existing capital markets — not USSR light.

playlists.title: Historical Socialism
playlists.description: USSR, Venezuela, and No True Scotsman defenses.

prompts.text: That was not real socialism. Real socialism has never been tried.
prompts.hint: Pin institutions: planning, property, political monopoly.

prompts.text: Venezuela only failed because of US sanctions.
prompts.hint: Pre-sanctions collapse, oil expropriation, and price controls.

playlists.title: Rapid Fire Intervention
playlists.description: Short rounds on minimum wage, rent control, and healthcare slogans.

prompts.text: Raising the minimum wage always helps the poor with no jobs lost.
prompts.hint: CBO ranges, hours, and youth employment.

prompts.text: Rent control is the obvious fix for housing costs.
Healthcare is a right so markets have no place in medicine.
prompts.hint: Supply, insurance vs care, and comparative systems.

playlists.title: Founding & Liberty
playlists.description: Constitution, natural rights, and collectivist redefinitions.

prompts.text: The Constitution is a living document that must evolve with socialist democracy.
prompts.hint: Written limits, amendment process, and majority tyranny.

prompts.text: Individual rights are secondary to collective liberation.
prompts.hint: Natural rights tradition vs class rights.
